India, heavily reliant on oil imports, faces significant economic challenges from these price increases.

Strong AI-driven data centre demand, record order book, robust earnings, capacity expansion and growing defence ambitions have prompted brokerages to turn bullish, with Deven Choksey seeing another 75% upside.

items.title: Why Swiggy shares fell over 5% today: Foreign ownership cap sparks $460 million passive outflow fears
items.summary: Swiggy shares fell after the company proposed cutting its foreign ownership limit to 49.5% from 100%, raising concerns over its eligibility for major global indices. The move could lead to Swiggy's exclusion from the MSCI Standard and FTSE indices, triggering an estimated $460 million in passive outflows, according to reports.

items.summary: Shares of HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank and Yes Bank have erased over Rs 1.5 lakh crore in market value this week following their June-quarter earnings. While investors reacted negatively to the results amid broader market weakness, brokerages say the sharp correction has made valuations more attractive, with Axis Bank emerging as a preferred pick.

items.title: Global Market: Japan's core inflation picks up in June; BOJ rate outlook keeps markets on edge
items.summary: Japan's core consumer inflation rose to 1.6% in June. This increase occurred while food prices eased and service inflation slowed. Underlying inflation, excluding food and fuel, also saw a slight decrease. Economists anticipate stronger inflationary pressures later this year due to rising energy costs. The Bank of Japan will consider these figures at its upcoming policy meeting.

items.title: Rekha Jhunjhunwala cuts Star Health stake to 3.04% from 15.5%; late Rakesh Jhunjhunwala promoter holding falls to nil
items.summary: Star Health and Allied Insurance Company's share price fell over 1% following Rekha Jhunjhunwala's significant stake reduction to 3.04%. Her sale of approximately 7.38 crore shares in FY27 resulted in the promoter holding dropping to nil by June 30, 2026.

items.title: Global Market: US urges BOJ to keep raising rates, flags persistent yen weakness despite narrower yield gap
items.summary: The US Treasury Department has urged the Bank of Japan to continue raising interest rates. This action aims to control inflation expectations and reduce excessive yen currency volatility. The yen has weakened significantly, reaching a 40-year low against the US dollar recently. Higher prices are impacting Japanese households' purchasing power despite wage gains. Further policy normalization by the Bank of Japan is seen as beneficial.

items.title: IndiGo shares sink 3% after Rs 238 crore Q1 loss, but why are Citi and Nuvama raising target prices?

Despite the earnings miss, brokerages including Citi and Nuvama retained their 'Buy' ratings and raised target prices, citing strong yields, pricing power, disciplined capacity expansion and long-term growth prospects driven by international expansion.

items.title: Why is market falling today? Sensex slumps over 800 points: 7 key factors behind Rs 3 lakh crore rout
items.summary: Indian stock markets experienced sharp losses for a fifth straight session on Friday. Sensex and Nifty indices saw significant drops, reflecting investor concerns. Oil prices exceeding $100 per barrel and FII selling fueled the downturn. Broader markets and specific sectors also registered substantial declines. This selloff erased over Rs 3 lakh crore from market capitalization.

items.title: Global Market: HSBC to sell Singapore insurance business to Allianz in $2.09 billion deal
items.summary: HSBC will sell its Singapore life and health insurance business to Allianz. This deal values the unit at S$2.7 billion and is expected to close in early 2027. The transaction will generate a pre-tax gain for HSBC and improve its capital ratio. Allianz gains a stronger presence in Singapore's insurance market through this acquisition. HSBC will distribute Allianz's insurance products in Singapore for fifteen years.

items.title: Sebi proposes shifting ODR oversight to MIIs for quicker grievance redressal
items.summary: Sebi proposes shifting online dispute resolution administration to market infrastructure institutions. This move aims to significantly speed up investor grievance redressal processes. Market infrastructure institutions will now handle conciliator and arbitrator appointments and oversight. Investors will have a voice in arbitrator selection, while conciliators are appointed directly. This framework change is estimated to shorten the overall resolution process by twenty-one days.

Economists anticipate further deficits in the coming days due to these factors.

Other precious metals like silver, platinum, and palladium also saw declines.

Logistics and semiconductors are also identified as key areas for future collaboration. This expansion aims to deepen the growing economic ties between the two nations. It aims to boost India's outbound shipments and global market access for sellers. The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade announced this policy review. This move will help increase India's exports without impacting small retailers.

Both nations also underscored concluding an interim bilateral trade arrangement.

"As soon as tomorrow, USTR will release its final responsive action on its Section 301 investigation of our top 60 trading partners' failure to address international trade in forced goods at their borders," he said.

items.title: ET Graphics: India is winning the battle against hunger, but not food costs
items.summary: India has achieved a 42% decline in undernourished people over two decades. The number of Indians unable to afford a healthy diet also fell significantly. This progress outpaces global trends in tackling hunger and malnutrition. However, the cost of a healthy diet has risen by over a third. These findings are based on a new report by the UN's Food and Agriculture Organization.

Profit for the June quarter saw a slight decrease compared to last year.

India's external sector remains steady with improving foreign investment inflows. The domestic economy navigated uncertainties well, supported by strong demand. Foreign exchange reserves are comfortable, covering over ten months of imports.

items.summary: Chirag Paswan said India must now shift from food self-reliance to value addition through processing, infrastructure, innovation and better farmer market access.

items.title: External sectors steady despite geopolitical uncertainties: RBI
items.summary: India's external sector shows steady improvement, boosted by foreign investment inflows. The economy remains strong despite global uncertainties and trade disruptions. Foreign exchange reserves provide ample cover for imports and external debt. Exports and imports saw strong growth in the first quarter of this fiscal year. Bilateral trade agreements will further enhance India's external trade momentum.

items.summary: It is one of the first publicly disclosed cyber-attacks carried out by AI without direct human involvement.

items.title: US-India trade deal could be signed in 3-4 months, US official says
items.summary: A U.S.-India trade agreement is nearing completion and could be signed soon. Negotiations are virtually finished, with only U.S. trade investigations pending resolution. These investigations cover unfair trade practices and may lead to retaliatory measures. Once completed, progress is expected for India and other nations. The agreement aims to expand market access and deepen economic ties between both countries.

items.title: Beyond roti, kapda, makaan: Inside India's new consumption story
items.summary: Indian consumers now prioritize experiences and lifestyle choices over basic needs. Average monthly spending has increased across both rural and urban areas. Food now forms a smaller portion of household expenditure, reflecting rising incomes. Younger generations balance saving for homes with immediate lifestyle purchases. Easy credit fuels consumption, increasing household debt levels significantly.

items.title: Economic activity hits 32-month high in June, says Icra; retains Q1 growth forecast at 6.4-6.6%
items.summary: Icra attributed the stronger momentum to the temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran, prolonged construction and mining activity due to a rainfall deficit, and favourable base effects

items.title: Karnataka attracts $13 billion in FDI as tech, GCC investments surge
items.summary: Karnataka's foreign direct investment nearly doubled to thirteen billion dollars last financial year. Tech investors and global capability centers significantly boosted Karnataka's investment inflows. Maharashtra, the top destination, experienced a dip in foreign direct investment after two years. Computer software and hardware sectors cornered nearly forty percent of total inflows. Food processing industry saw foreign direct investment grow nearly six times.

items.title: Sugar stock limits may offer temporary relief but risk disrupting the supply chain: Industry
items.summary: India is unlikely to face any immediate sugar shortage, as domestic stocks are expected to be sufficient to meet demand until the next crushing season begins, says Atul Chaturvedi of Shree Renuka Sugars.

items.title: RBI likely used dollar deposit surge to cut record FX forward book, economists say
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India likely used deposit inflows to reduce its forward book. This action helped the central bank manage its near-term dollar obligations effectively. Economists suggest this strategy offset short-dollar positions in the forward market. Inflows attracted over twenty billion dollars, with most from foreign currency deposits. June data will provide further evidence of this reduction in forward liabilities.

items.title: Cabinet clears ₹62,500 cr MPMS scheme: 'Focus now on components, design, research'
India's edible oil imports to surge July-October as supplies shrink before festivals
items.summary: India's edible oil imports will increase from July to October. This rise is due to slower domestic soybean and rapeseed crushing operations. Higher imports are expected to meet demand before the festive season begins. Refiners are actively purchasing palm and soy oil for upcoming shipments. This will support global vegetable oil futures and reduce supplier inventories.

items.title: 200% Trump tariff shock? India’s cheap pills may still beat US rivals
items.summary: US President Donald Trump's proposed tariffs of up to 200% on imported generic medicines could put pressure on India's pharmaceutical exporters, but the country's cost advantage may help many drugs remain competitive in the US market, according to the Global Trade Research Initiative (GTRI).

items.title: Will your job be replaced by AI? Here are the roles most affected
items.summary: Artificial Intelligence companies make vast claims about their tools replacing human labour - our charts show what's happening.

items.title: Why are central banks buying more gold? Here's what it means for the global economy
items.summary: Central Banks and Gold: Central banks globally are buying gold at a record pace. This trend is driven by geopolitical risks and inflation concerns. Gold offers diversification and a hedge against currency devaluation. Many nations are also bringing their gold reserves home. This shift signals a long-term strategy for financial resilience.

items.title: Trump’s 100% generic drug duty threatens US low-cost supply
items.summary: President Donald Trump has given generic drugmakers two years to shift manufacturing to the US or face 100% import tariffs from August 2028, rising to 200% a year later. The move threatens India's pharmaceutical exports, could disrupt low-cost medicine supplies, raise drug prices, and trigger shortages for millions of American patients.

items.title: Trump's Generic Drug Tariffs Explained: What it means for Indian pharma before the 200% storm
items.summary: Trump's generic drug tariffs plan provides Indian drugmakers a two-year duty-free window. This allows companies time to reassess manufacturing and supply chain strategies. After this period, tariffs will escalate significantly, impacting export economics. Several Indian firms already have US manufacturing operations for flexibility. The next two years will shape how India serves its largest overseas pharmaceutical market.
